Title: Genome-wide association mapping within a local Arabidopsis thaliana population more fully reveals the genetic architecture for defensive metabolite diversity
Journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B: Biological Sciences

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in methionine chain elongation steps affecting glucosinolate side-chain length; associated with variation in intermediate-length aliphatic glucosinolates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor structure for target product diversity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Natural polymorphism at BCAT3 alters abundances of aliphatic glucosinolates with intermediate-length side chains.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BCAT3 was identified as a novel GWAS-associated biosynthetic locus in the TOU-A population. 2. The locus affected abundances of aliphatic glucosinolates with intermediate-length side chains. 3. Its inferred effects matched previous BCAT3 knockout mutant phenotypes, supporting BCAT3 as the causal candidate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS in local population; comparison of SNP effect patterns with prior knockout mutant data and known biochemical pathway position
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Chain-elongated precursors contributing to intermediate-length aliphatic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (HPLC-MS/MS glucosinolate profiling); whole-genome resequencing-derived SNP genotypes from published dataset
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modify aliphatic glucosinolate side-chain distribution in Brassicales crops.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes BCAT3 as providing a previously undescribed natural variation axis distinct from the better-known MAM locus.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes condensation reactions in the methionine chain-elongation cycle that determine side-chain length of aliphatic glucosinolates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor structure for target product diversity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for side-chain length variation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MAM locus shapes chain-length distribution of aliphatic glucosinolates, especially short- versus long-chain products depending on paralog.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The MAM locus was significantly associated with aliphatic glucosinolate variation in TOU-A and in broad mapping panels. 2. The inferred effects matched known functions of MAM1/MAM2 in short-chain and MAM3 in long-chain glucosinolate production. 3. The locus remains a major determinant of aliphatic glucosinolate chemotype variation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; comparison with previous functional genetic and biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Short- or long-chain methionine-derived glucosinolate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major target for engineering glucosinolate side-chain composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses the MAM1/3 locus; exact causal paralog among MAM1/MAM2/MAM3 was not resolved within this GWAS peak.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core structure synthesis from chain-elongated amino acid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chain-elongated methionine derivatives toward aldoxime intermediates in aliphatic glucosinolate core biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for formation of core pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as strong pathway flux determinant, especially CYP79F2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Natural variation at the CYP79F locus strongly alters aliphatic glucosinolate pathway flux, particularly affecting shortest side-chain molecules.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A novel association was detected at the paralogous CYP79F1/CYP79F2 locus in TOU-A. 2. The leading SNP had especially large effects on short-chain aliphatic glucosinolates. 3. The effect pattern is consistent with prior evidence that CYP79F2 strongly controls pathway flux and propyl glucosinolate production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; concordance with previous biochemical and functional genetic studies; comparison with prior GWAS/QTL in Arabidopsis and other Brassicaceae
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Aldoxime intermediates for aliphatic glucosinolate core formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to modify overall flux and short-chain aliphatic glucosinolate composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper could not resolve whether CYP79F1, CYP79F2, or both underlie the association.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts downstream of CYP79F in aliphatic glucosinolate core biosynthesis to process oxime intermediates toward glucosinolate formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for formation of core pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Natural polymorphism at CYP83A1 contributes to aliphatic glucosinolate profile variation in TOU-A.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP83A1 was identified as a previously unreported association in TOU-A. 2. The locus lies downstream of MAM and BCAT3 in aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis. 3. Its candidacy is supported by concordance between pathway position and observed metabolite effects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; interpretation using pathway logic and prior biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Activated intermediates for glucosinolate core synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for tuning core biosynthetic flux, though direct causal polymorphism remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The association was novel in natural variation studies according to the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes S-oxygenation of methylthioalkyl glucosinolates to methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Variation at GS-OX1 is associated with shifts between methylthioalkyl and methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolate forms.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GS-OX1 was detected as an associated locus in TOU-A and had been missed in previous large European GWAS. 2. The locus showed strong association signals, especially when slightly rarer variants were included. 3. Effects suggest contribution to methylthioalkyl versus methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolate balance, though linkage disequilibrium or unexpected effects may contribute.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; pathway-informed interpretation; principal component GWAS for opposing methylthioalkyl versus methylsulfinylalkyl shifts
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for fine-tuning health- and defense-relevant sulfur oxidation states of aliphatic glucosinolates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes that strongest associations did not always map most clearly to direct precursor:product ratios, leaving some mechanistic ambiguity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes side-chain modification of methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates to alkenyl or hydroxyalkyl derivatives depending on allele/paralog state.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms downstream structural variants of aliphatic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for glucosinolate structural chemotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOP polymorphism alters aliphatic glucosinolate structural profiles in TOU-A, mainly through quantitative effects on precursor abundance rather than complete product loss.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The AOP locus was significantly associated with aliphatic glucosinolate variation in TOU-A and broader panels. 2. In TOU-A, the polymorphism caused quantitative shifts rather than the presence/absence patterns typical of broad-scale loss-of-function alleles. 3. This highlights local functional variation distinct from large-effect loss-of-function chemotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; comparison with prior functional genetic evidence and known enzyme function
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modifying glucosinolate structural chemotype without abolishing pathway function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific causal paralog not resolved; paper refers broadly to the AOP locus, historically AOP2/3.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxylation of alkenyl glucosinolates to hydroxyalkenyl glucosinolates.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for hydroxyalkenyl glucosinolate production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GS-OH polymorphism changes precursor and product abundances, quantitatively altering hydroxyalkenyl glucosinolate levels in TOU-A.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GS-OH was significantly associated with aliphatic glucosinolate variation in TOU-A and broad mapping panels. 2. Unlike broad-scale loss-of-function alleles that abolish products, TOU-A alleles caused quantitative shifts in precursor and product levels. 3. The locus acts at the final secondary modification step of aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; concordance with previous functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for adjusting final hydroxyalkenyl glucosinolate composition in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes that local alleles are functional and avoid the masking seen with widespread loss-of-function alleles.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxylation of indol-3-ylmethyl glucosinolate at the 4-position, leading to 4-hydroxy-I3M and subsequently 4-methoxy-I3M derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to methoxylated indolic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for 4-substituted indolic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CYP81F2 polymorphism alters levels of 4-hydroxy-I3M-GSL and 4-methoxy-I3M-GSL.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP81F2 was one of three indolic glucosinolate loci associated in TOU-A. 2. The locus affected 4-hydroxy-I3M-GSL and its derivative 4-methoxy-I3M-GSL. 3. The GWAS effect pattern matched previous QTL mapping and functional validation of CYP81F2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single-trait, ratio-trait, and multi-trait GWAS; concordance with previous QTL and functional validation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target to tune specific 4-substituted indolic glucosinolate derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP81F2 was already functionally validated in prior work cited by the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Paralogous CYP81F enzymes catalyze an alternative hydroxylation step on indol-3-ylmethyl glucosinolate, generating the precursor to 1-methoxy-I3M-GSL.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for 1-methoxy indolic glucosinolate branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Variation at the CYP81F1/3/4 locus affects 1-methoxy-I3M-GSL accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The CYP81F1/CYP81F3/CYP81F4 locus was associated with indolic glucosinolate variation in TOU-A. 2. The locus specifically affected the branch producing 1-methoxy-I3M-GSL. 3. The result supports differential in planta roles of CYP81F paralog loci despite overlapping in vitro substrate specificities.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-trait GWAS; comparison with previous mapping and biochemical studies
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering branch-specific indolic glucosinolate profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The immediate hydroxylated product is unstable and was not directly observed in the profiling data; the association was inferred via 1-methoxy-I3M-GSL.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Secondary methoxylation of hydroxylated indolic glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyze O-methoxylation of hydroxy-I3M glucosinolates; in planta evidence here supports a role mainly in 4-hydroxy-I3M to 4-methoxy-I3M conversion.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms methoxylated indolic glucosinolate product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Natural variation at the IGMT tandem array affects 4-hydroxy-I3M abundance and supports a role in 4-methoxy-I3M production in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The IGMT locus was newly associated with indolic glucosinolate variation in TOU-A. 2. The association peaked directly in the tandem array of IGMT paralogs. 3. The effect pattern supports in planta specialization of IGMT1-4 for 4-hydroxy-I3M methoxylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-trait GWAS; concordance with prior biochemical assays and IGMT5 knockout interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; rosettes harvested at 21 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for selectively altering methoxylated indolic glucosinolate composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper specifically refers to a tandem array of IGMT1-4; IGMT5 is discussed as prior background evidence, not as a mapped core gene.
